metadata.description: Claude Code plugins published by marsman-io. A family of phase-aware diagnostic-skill plugins — each ships read-only skills that critique a domain (design, project management, …) through grounded conceptual lenses + a lifecycle taxonomy, with an orchestrator skill that detects phase and composes the right leaf-skill chain — plus standalone skill collections.

plugins.name: design-loop-methodology
plugins.description: Phase-aware diagnostic skills that critique UI / design-system surfaces through five conceptual lenses (loop, intent, constraint, hierarchy, lifecycle). The design-loop orchestrator skill detects bootstrap/apply/audit/tweak/refactor and composes the right leaf-skill chain; single-lens requests auto-route to the leaf skills directly. Ships 12 composable skills plus a design-system-audit agent that sweeps a whole design system for cross-screen drift in an isolated pass.

plugins.name: design-craft-skills
plugins.description: Two generative design-craft skills for building and polishing real interfaces — ui-designer (visual craft: spacing, color, typography, design tokens, component systems, pixel-perfect polish) and ux-designer (experience strategy: user flows, information architecture, interaction design, usability, the psychology behind decisions). Each ships progressive-disclosure reference files and auto-routes on its own triggers; the two hand off (flow → visual). Generative, not diagnostic — the build-and-refine counterpart to design-loop-methodology's read-only critique.

plugins.name: project-loop-methodology
plugins.description: Phase-aware diagnostic skills that critique agentic product work through PM-derived lenses (loop, outcome altitude, constraint, priority budget, intent verification, lifecycle). The project-loop orchestrator skill detects charter/plan/execute/adjust/replan and composes the right leaf-skill chain; single-lens requests auto-route to the leaf skills directly. Ships 14 composable skills plus a project-work-audit agent that sweeps a whole workstream for cross-feature drift in an isolated pass — for indie product builders checking whether agent-built work matches intent.

plugins.name: meta-prompt-skills
plugins.description: A flat collection of seven standalone meta-prompt skills: humanizer (strip AI tells from prose), video-distill (video → layered notes), sota-scan (benchmark a project against top peers, every gap names a real project), session-mine (mine recent transcripts for recurring corrections), clarify-deep (gather context then ask deep blind-spot questions), book-to-skill (turn a non-fiction book's method into installable skills), and prompt-improver (improve an existing prompt/skill in auto or interactive mode). No agents — each skill picks its own mode where relevant and auto-routes on its own triggers.

plugins.name: indie-founder-lenses
plugins.description: Four phase-aware, read-only diagnostic lens skills for the surfaces a solo founder lives on — launch-readiness-lens, founder-focus-budget, pricing-page-lens, and conversion-intent-lens — each detecting its own phase and critiquing one founder surface (launch readiness, founder focus, pricing, landing-page conversion) against a cited artifact. Not a loop and not a methodology: no orchestrator, no shared lifecycle, no persona — independent lens skills (each composing its own leaf skills) that accept in-repo or pasted/external artifacts. No agents.

plugins.name: release-flow-skills
plugins.description: A collection of git / PR / release-lifecycle skills for shipping software; the first is changelog-from-diff, which turns a git diff into a clean, grouped changelog / release-notes entry, user-invoked and never rewriting history.

plugins.name: frontier-analysis-skills
plugins.description: A flat collection of five codebase-neutral skills for spending an expensive frontier reasoning model well — seam-conformance, layer-trace, contract-drift, boundary-judgment, and invariant-capture — each gathering evidence cheaply, then making one focused reasoning call, then capturing the verdict as a durable rule + test so you spend once and enforce forever. No agents; each skill auto-routes on its own triggers.

plugins.name: experience-mapping-skills
plugins.description: Ten codebase-neutral, generative skills for mapping the experience of a multi-surface developer tool (CLI, SDK, REST API, MCP server, dashboard, installer, docs) — experience-map, journey-map, service-blueprint, current-future-state, jtbd-map, touchpoint-inventory, ecosystem-map, concept-terminology-audit, surface-information-architecture, and cross-surface-synthesis. Each produces one structured artifact (map / matrix / inventory) grounded in an established UX method and adapted for dev tools. No agents; each skill stands alone and auto-routes on its own triggers. Pairs with dx-evaluation-skills: map first, then evaluate.

plugins.name: dx-evaluation-skills
plugins.description: Eleven codebase-neutral, read-only skills for evaluating the developer experience of a multi-surface tool — task-analysis, cognitive-walkthrough, heuristic-evaluation (Nielsen's 10 + a DX supplement), golden-path, the flagship failure-state-matrix, experience-principles, and the per-surface conformance audits cli-conformance, api-error-model-audit, sdk-ergonomics, mcp-server-ux, and installer-upgrade-audit. Each diagnoses and emits a structured artifact — you intervene. Built on the conviction that for dev tools failure UX outweighs happy-path and agents are first-class users. No agents; each skill auto-routes on its own triggers. Pairs with experience-mapping-skills: map first, then evaluate.

plugins.name: developer-docs-skills
plugins.description: Ten codebase-neutral skills for writing and auditing the documentation of a multi-surface developer tool (CLI, SDK, REST API, MCP server, dashboard, installer, docs), built on Diátaxis — every page is one of tutorial / how-to / reference / explanation and mixing modes is the primary documentation failure. Ships the flagship docs-diataxis-audit (classify every page, flag mode-mixing, find missing quadrants), four generative mode-builders quickstart-builder (install->first-call with a time-to-first-success target), tutorial-scaffold (learning-oriented), how-to-recipe (task-oriented), and explanation-doc (understanding-oriented), reference-from-spec (machine-generated reference from an OpenAPI spec / --help dump / type signatures), and the read-only critics readme-audit (standard-readme + Make a README), doc-surface-drift (docs vs the actual surfaces, partly machine-checkable), style-conformance (Google dev-docs style guide), and code-sample-audit (runnable, copy-pasteable, idempotent samples). Grounded in Diátaxis, the Google style guide, Write the Docs, and standard-readme. No agents; each skill auto-routes on its own triggers. Completes the trio with experience-mapping-skills and dx-evaluation-skills: map, evaluate, then document.
